q: Sound is a sequence of pressure waves which propagates through ___ media such as air or water.
During their propagation, waves can be reflected, refracted or ___ by the medium.
A relationship between density and pressure, affected by ___, determines the speed of sound within the medium.
The ___ of the medium determines the rate at which sound is attenuated.
For many media, such as air or water, attenuation due to viscosity is ___.
Speed of a sound wave can also be affected by the density, temperature, and ___ of the medium.
In a solid medium, the speed of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{Y}{\rho}}$, where $Y$ is ___.
In liquids, the velocity of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}}$, where $B$ is the ___.
In gases, the velocity of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma p}{\rho}}$, where $\gamma$ is the ___ of gas.
Longitudinal sound waves travel faster in solids than they do in liquids than they do in ___.
Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are ___ properties of the medium.
The level of intensity of a sound is measured relative to a reference intensity value taken as ___.
The reference intensity corresponds to the minimum intensity of sound that can be heard (___).
The decibel (dB) is the unit used for measuring sound ___.
For every $10 \text{ dB}$ added to the sound level, the loudness of the sound is increased by ___ times.
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the ___ reaching an eardrum measured in $\text{W m}^{-2}$.
Echoes are produced where a sound wave is reflected from a ___ surface.
The echo or the reflected sound can be heard separately from the original sound if the source of the sound is much ___ to the observer than to the reflecting surface.
To reduce the effect of echo in buildings, walls are covered up with ___.
These padding materials absorb the incident sound and reduce the amount of sound reflecting off ___ surfaces.
Echo can also be used to determine the distance $d$ between a source of sound and the reflecting surface by using formula, ___.
Some animals such as bats are known to use ___ to navigate and locate their prey.
Echolocation is the special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the ___.
A reverberation is a reflection of light or sound waves, or a wide reaching impact of an ___.
An example of a reverberation is the sound ___ around in a large speaker.
Audibility means the ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in certain range of ___.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit is $20 \text{ Hz}$ and the upper limit is about ___.
By using ___ generator, the audible frequency range can be determined.
Low frequency sounds which are under the lower limit of audibility ($20 \text{ Hz}$) are called ___.
High frequency sounds which are above the upper limit of audibility ($20\ 000 \text{ Hz}$) are called ___.
Bats and whales use ultrasound in echolocation and most ___ technologies use ultrasound.
Manufacturers of concrete use ultrasound to check for cracks or ___.
A prenatal ultrasound test uses high-frequency sound waves, inaudible to the human ear, that are transmitted through the abdomen via a device called a ___.
Ultrasonic scalers use ultrasound to remove calculus (plaque) deposits from the ___.
The transmission of high energy ultrasound may result in the certain of ___ bubbles.
Cavitation bubbles are created at sites of ___.
The sonar (sound navigation and ranging) is a type of technology that works based on ___.
Infrasound waves have long wavelengths, and are thus able to travel over ___ distances.
The Doppler effect is the change in frequency of a wave related to an observer due to the ___ motion between the source and observer.
A common example of Doppler shift is the change of frequency (pitch) heard when a vehicle sounding a ___ approach and recedes from an observer.
The Doppler equation $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$ includes $v$ which represents:
a: speed of sound in the medium
